page.title: Marbella Sunset Tapas and Wine Cruise on a Private Yacht

data.meta_description: Book a Marbella sunset tapas and wine cruise on a private yacht from Puerto Banús. 2-hour charter from €749 with skipper, drinks & snacks included.
data.summary: A sunset tapas and wine cruise in Marbella offers a private yacht charter from Puerto Banús with Spanish wine, local tapas, and stunning coastal views. Prices start at €749 for 2 hours, including skipper, drinks, and snacks.
key_facts: Includes licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA
Onboard tapas & Spanish wine, cava, beer, soft drinks
Max 9–11 guests depending on yacht (Astondoa 40: 9 pax, Azimut 39: 11 pax)
Views of La Concha, Golden Mile, and Marbella coastline
Flexible booking via WhatsApp for other yacht options

faq.q: What is included in a Marbella sunset tapas and wine cruise?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. For the sunset cruise, we also serve a selection of Spanish tapas and local wines to complement the evening.

faq.q: How much does a sunset tapas and wine cruise cost?
faq.a: The 2-hour sunset cruise on our Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 starts at €749 for up to 9 or 11 guests respectively. This includes all drinks, tapas, skipper, and taxes. Longer durations or larger yachts are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which yachts are available for the sunset cruise?
faq.a: Our standard sunset cruise uses the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax). For larger groups, you can upgrade to a Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719 for 4 hours) or other fleet boats. Contact us via WhatsApp for options.

faq.q: Where does the sunset cruise depart from?
faq.a: All cruises depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early for boarding. The exact berth is confirmed upon booking.

faq.q: What is the typical itinerary for a sunset cruise?
faq.a: After leaving Puerto Banús, we cruise west along the coast towards Estepona, passing the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina. We anchor near Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for sunset views, serving tapas and wine as the sun dips behind La Concha. Return to port after 2 hours.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food or drinks?
faq.a: Our package already includes Spanish tapas, wine, cava, beer, and soft drinks — enough for the duration. You are welcome to bring extra snacks if you have dietary needs, but we kindly ask no outside alcohol.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Safety comes first. If conditions are unsafe (high winds, storms), we will reschedule your cruise or offer a full refund. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

faq.q: How do I book a sunset tapas and wine cruise?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and personalised quotes. A deposit secures your slot, with the balance due on the day.

data.body_html:
Imagine gliding out of Puerto Banús as the sun begins to dip behind La Concha, a glass of chilled Spanish wine in hand, and a plate of jamón ibérico and manchego within reach. That is the essence of a Marbella sunset tapas and wine cruise on a private yacht — an intimate two-hour escape along the Costa del Sol, designed for couples, small groups, or anyone craving a golden-hour experience without the crowds. Our fleet includes the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 guests) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 guests), both departing from Puerto Banús and priced from €749 for two hours, including skipper, fuel, drinks, tapas, and all taxes.

Why Choose a Sunset Tapas and Wine Cruise in Marbella?

A sunset cruise offers the best of Marbella’s coastline in a compact, relaxing format. You avoid the midday heat, enjoy softer light for photos, and watch the sea turn amber as the city lights begin to twinkle. Unlike a standard boat hire, this experience is curated around food and wine — perfect for a romantic date, anniversary, or a sophisticated evening with friends. The combination of local Spanish wines and traditional tapas elevates the trip beyond a simple boat ride into a culinary mini-adventure.

Our Yachts for the Sunset Cruise

We offer two primary vessels for this experience, both based in Puerto Banús and fully equipped for sunset cruising:

  • Astondoa 40 (12.5m) — sleeps 9 guests, €749 for 2 hours. A sleek Spanish-built motor yacht with a spacious aft deck perfect for dining al fresco.
  • Azimut 39 (12.5m) — sleeps 11 guests, €749 for 2 hours. An Italian classic with a large cockpit and comfortable saloon for cooler evenings.

Both yachts include a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and a full drinks package (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava). The tapas selection is prepared fresh and served onboard. For larger groups, consider upgrading to a Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) from €4,719 for 4 hours, which also includes a jet ski.

What’s Included in Your Sunset Cruise

Every charter comes with a standard set of inclusions, so you know exactly what you are paying for:

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full duration
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks and a curated tapas platter
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%) — no hidden fees

No extra charges for ice, glasses, or crew gratuities (though tips are appreciated for good service). The price is all-inclusive from the moment you step aboard.

Typical Sunset Itinerary

Your cruise begins at Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina lined with superyachts and boutiques. Heading west, you will pass the Golden Mile, Marbella Marina, and the iconic La Concha mountain. The skipper will anchor near Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés — calm coves with uninterrupted sunset views. As the sun sets, tapas and wine are served. The return to port is a gentle cruise under the evening sky, arriving back after approximately two hours.

What Tapas and Wines Are Served?

Our tapas selection changes seasonally but typically includes:

  • Jamón ibérico de bellota
  • Manchego cheese with membrillo
  • Patatas bravas with aioli
  • Pan con tomate
  • Olives and almonds

Wines are Spanish — a crisp Albariño from Rías Baixas or a fruity Rioja Crianza. Cava is offered for toasts. All drinks are included, so you can enjoy freely without worrying about a bar tab.

Who Is This Cruise For?

The sunset tapas and wine cruise is ideal for:

  • Couples seeking a romantic evening
  • Small groups of friends celebrating a birthday or reunion
  • Business clients or team-building events
  • Proposals or anniversaries (we can arrange flowers or champagne on request)

Children are welcome, but the focus is on an adult-oriented experience. The yacht size limits intimacy — you will not be sharing with strangers.

How to Book Your Sunset Cruise

Booking is straightforward. Visit our Boat Rental Marbella homepage to check availability or contact us via WhatsApp for personalised advice. A 50% deposit secures your date, with the balance due on the day. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during peak summer months (June–September) when sunset slots fill quickly. For larger groups or custom durations, get in touch and we will tailor a package.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in a Marbella sunset tapas and wine cruise?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, Spanish IVA (21%), water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. For the sunset cruise, we also serve a selection of Spanish tapas and local wines to complement the evening.

How much does a sunset tapas and wine cruise cost?

The 2-hour sunset cruise on our Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 starts at €749 for up to 9 or 11 guests respectively. This includes all drinks, tapas, skipper, and taxes. Longer durations or larger yachts are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which yachts are available for the sunset cruise?

Our standard sunset cruise uses the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax). For larger groups, you can upgrade to a Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, from €4,719 for 4 hours) or other fleet boats. Contact us via WhatsApp for options.

Where does the sunset cruise depart from?

All cruises depart from Puerto Banús, the iconic marina in Marbella. We recommend arriving 15 minutes early for boarding. The exact berth is confirmed upon booking.

What is the typical itinerary for a sunset cruise?

After leaving Puerto Banús, we cruise west along the coast towards Estepona, passing the Golden Mile and Marbella Marina. We anchor near Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for sunset views, serving tapas and wine as the sun dips behind La Concha. Return to port after 2 hours.

Can I bring my own food or drinks?

Our package already includes Spanish tapas, wine, cava, beer, and soft drinks — enough for the duration. You are welcome to bring extra snacks if you have dietary needs, but we kindly ask no outside alcohol.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Safety comes first. If conditions are unsafe (high winds, storms), we will reschedule your cruise or offer a full refund. The skipper makes the final call on the day.

How do I book a sunset tapas and wine cruise?

You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and personalised quotes. A deposit secures your slot, with the balance due on the day.

Ready to experience the magic of a Marbella sunset? Book your sunset tapas and wine cruise now and enjoy an unforgettable evening on the Mediterranean.
